pages.raw_text:
The trouble occasioned by the at
tempt of the stationary engineers to
send delegates to both trades assem-
blies has resulted in the resignation
of John Hunt as secretary and treas
urer of the Falls City Trades and
Labor assembly.


The stationary engineers We're or
dered by the international body to
join the trades and labor assembly,
to which they sent delegates


But, fearing a fight between them
and the brewers, the engineers de-
clared they would also remain MEM
bers of the Spokane Trades council
with which the brewers are affiliated.


Secretary Hunt of the new council,
as a matter of principle, refused to
represent the engineers further. He
gave notice that the engineers had
violated the International orders and
that hereafter he would not pay his
dues to the local body, but would
forward them to the international
secretary and also resign as dele
gate and officer of the local.


Because of the veteran officers
standing in labor circles the action
has created something of sensation.
